steps.title: Use the exponent rule
steps.content:
An exponent of $\frac{1}{2}$ means the square root of the entire base:
$$a^{\frac{1}{2}}=\sqrt{a}$$

steps.title: Apply it to the expression
steps.content:
The base is the entire product $174y$, so:
$$(174y)^{\frac{1}{2}}=\sqrt{174y}$$

steps.title: Match the equivalent form
steps.content: The equivalent expression is $\sqrt{174y}$, matching the option that keeps the square root over the whole product. The answer is C.
